query: What is the current level of crude oil in the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve as of June 2026, per EIA weekly data (WCSSTUS1)? What has been the weekly trajectory over the past 3-6 months (fills or releases)?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**Current SPR Level (June 2026):** Per EIA weekly series WCSSTUS1, the SPR stood at **~325.7 million barrels** for the last June reporting week (ending June 26, 2026), falling to ~319.5 million by July 3 — the **lowest level since April 1983**.

**Trajectory (past 3–6 months):** The data shows a dramatic reversal from fills to releases:
- **Jan–Mar 2026:** Stable/slightly rising, peaking at **415.4M barrels** in mid-March.
- **Late Mar–July 2026:** Steep, sustained **drawdown** — declining ~89.8M barrels (−21.6%) from the March peak to end-June, with weekly releases of 5.5M–10M barrels.

**Cause (critical context):** This is an **emergency coordinated release**, not routine market activity. The U.S. announced a planned **172-million-barrel release** (largest single-country release in history) as part of a coordinated **IEA 400M-barrel release across 32 nations**, responding to Middle East conflict / **Strait of Hormuz closure**. It's structured as an exchange (barrels to be returned later).

All findings were cross-validated across EIA official data (WCSSTUS1 series + EIA Today in Energy) and multiple news sources. The full structured output with data points and sources has been submitted.

sources.title: Weekly U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in SPR (Thousand Barrels)
sources.snippet: Weekly SPR ending stocks series WCSSTUS1; June 2026 values 349,192 (06/05) to 325,655 (06/26); July 3 at 319,489.

sources.title: DOE has released 17.5 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve since March
sources.snippet: 17.5M barrels released between week ending March 20 and April 24; SPR at 397.9M barrels; 172M barrel total planned release; coordinated IEA 400M barrel release; structured as an exchange.

sources.title: SPR Level History: From 727M Peak to Post-Drawdown Refill
sources.snippet: As of April 10, 2026, the US SPR holds 409.2 million barrels — 44% below its December 2009 peak of 727 million.

sources.title: Strategic Petroleum Reserve Hits 1983 Low
sources.snippet: SPR has fallen to its lowest level since April 1983 after months of war-related releases.

sources.title: MRP 326: Running on Empty: The U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve
sources.snippet: In mid-March 2026 the administration announced 172 million barrel release, part of coordinated 400-million-barrel release by 32 nations in response to closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
